title: HOUSING STABILIZATION SERVICES - 211info
text: OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION COOS HOUSING STABILIZATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers coordinated entry services for housing. Assistance may include help with rent deposits, rental assistance, or eviction prevention. Households may receive one-time assistance within a 12-month period. Assistance depends on eligibility, funding, and program requirements. 1855 Thomas Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: To be considered, households must generally: Meet income eligibility requirements. Income limits vary by funding source and are usually based on Area Median Income (AMI). Be experiencing a housing crisis, such as: being homeless OR being at risk of homelessness (for example, an eviction notice) OR actively fleeing domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking. (Documentation is required.) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 435-7080 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 435-7080 Main phone (541) 435-7754 Main phone South Coast Food Share (541) 888-3717 Administration South Coast Head Start Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website or walk in to complete a Housing Stabilization Services Application FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Completed Housing Stabilization Services Application and required documents, which may include: -Proof of income or no income -Lease, eviction notice, or landlord verification -Identification and household information -Other documents required by specific funding programs LANGUAGES: English, Spanish interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Coos County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located in the same complex as Coos Health and Wellness and Advanced Health. Services: Eviction Prevention Assistance Housing Related Coordinated Entry Rent Payment Assistance Rental Deposit Assistance Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - EARLY HEAD START EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - HEAD START HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - MEDNET HOUSING/SHELTER - WEATHERIZATION ASSISTANCE HUMAN REPRODUCTION - FAMILY SUPPORT AND CONNECTIONS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES JUDICIAL SERVICES - COURT APPOINTED SPECIAL ADVOCATES UTILITIES - UTILITY ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION COOS
